4788 * as the estimated range for each byte, after some fudging to deal with
4789 * the fact that we probably aren't going to see the full range that way.
4790 *
4791 * An additional refinement is that we discard any common prefix of the
4792 * three strings before computing the scaled values.  This allows us to
4793 * "zoom in" when we encounter a narrow data range.  An example is a phone
4794 * number database where all the values begin with the same area code.
4795 * (Actually, the bounds will be adjacent histogram-bin-boundary values,
4796 * so this is more likely to happen than you might think.)
4797 */
4798static void
4799convert_string_to_scalar(char *value,
4800						 double *scaledvalue,
4801						 char *lobound,
4802						 double *scaledlobound,
4803						 char *hibound,
4804						 double *scaledhibound)
4805{
4806	int			rangelo,
4807				rangehi;
4808	char	   *sptr;
4809
4810	rangelo = rangehi = (unsigned char) hibound[0];
4811	for (sptr = lobound; *sptr; sptr++)
4812	{
4813		if (rangelo > (unsigned char) *sptr)
4814			rangelo = (unsigned char) *sptr;
4815		if (rangehi < (unsigned char) *sptr)
4816			rangehi = (unsigned char) *sptr;
4817	}
4818	for (sptr = hibound; *sptr; sptr++)
4819	{
4820		if (rangelo > (unsigned char) *sptr)
4821			rangelo = (unsigned char) *sptr;
4822		if (rangehi < (unsigned char) *sptr)
4823			rangehi = (unsigned char) *sptr;
4824	}
4825	/* If range includes any upper-case ASCII chars, make it include all */
4826	if (rangelo <= 'Z' && rangehi >= 'A')
4827	{
4828		if (rangelo > 'A')
4829			rangelo = 'A';
4830		if (rangehi < 'Z')
4831			rangehi = 'Z';
4832	}
4833	/* Ditto lower-case */
4834	if (rangelo <= 'z' && rangehi >= 'a')
4835	{
4836		if (rangelo > 'a')
4837			rangelo = 'a';
4838		if (rangehi < 'z')
4839			rangehi = 'z';
4840	}
4841	/* Ditto digits */
4842	if (rangelo <= '9' && rangehi >= '0')
4843	{
4844		if (rangelo > '0')
4845			rangelo = '0';
4846		if (rangehi < '9')
4847			rangehi = '9';
4848	}
4849
4850	/*
4851	 * If range includes less than 10 chars, assume we have not got enough
4852	 * data, and make it include regular ASCII set.
4853	 */
4854	if (rangehi - rangelo < 9)
4855	{
4856		rangelo = ' ';
4857		rangehi = 127;
4858	}
4859
4860	/*
4861	 * Now strip any common prefix of the three strings.
4862	 */
4863	while (*lobound)
4864	{
4865		if (*lobound != *hibound || *lobound != *value)
4866			break;
4867		lobound++, hibound++, value++;
4868	}
4869
4870	/*
4871	 * Now we can do the conversions.
4872	 */
4873	*scaledvalue = convert_one_string_to_scalar(value, rangelo, rangehi);
4874	*scaledlobound = convert_one_string_to_scalar(lobound, rangelo, rangehi);
4875	*scaledhibound = convert_one_string_to_scalar(hibound, rangelo, rangehi);
4876}
4877
4878static double
4879convert_one_string_to_scalar(char *value, int rangelo, int rangehi)
4880{
4881	int			slen = strlen(value);
4882	double		num,
4883				denom,
4884				base;
4885
4886	if (slen <= 0)
4887		return 0.0;				/* empty string has scalar value 0 */
4888
4889	/*
4890	 * There seems little point in considering more than a dozen bytes from
4891	 * the string.  Since base is at least 10, that will give us nominal
4892	 * resolution of at least 12 decimal digits, which is surely far more
4893	 * precision than this estimation technique has got anyway (especially in
4894	 * non-C locales).  Also, even with the maximum possible base of 256, this
4895	 * ensures denom cannot grow larger than 256^13 = 2.03e31, which will not
4896	 * overflow on any known machine.
4897	 */
4898	if (slen > 12)
4899		slen = 12;
4900
4901	/* Convert initial characters to fraction */
4902	base = rangehi - rangelo + 1;
4903	num = 0.0;
4904	denom = base;
4905	while (slen-- > 0)
4906	{
4907		int			ch = (unsigned char) *value++;
4908
4909		if (ch < rangelo)
4910			ch = rangelo - 1;
4911		else if (ch > rangehi)
4912			ch = rangehi + 1;
4913		num += ((double) (ch - rangelo)) / denom;
4914		denom *= base;
4915	}
4916
4917	return num;
4918}
4919
4920/*
4921 * Convert a string-type Datum into a palloc'd, null-terminated string.
4922 *
4923 * On failure (e.g., unsupported typid), set *failure to true;
4924 * otherwise, that variable is not changed.  (We'll return NULL on failure.)
4925 *
4926 * When using a non-C locale, we must pass the string through pg_strxfrm()
4927 * before continuing, so as to generate correct locale-specific results.
4928 */
4929static char *
4930convert_string_datum(Datum value, Oid typid, Oid collid, bool *failure)
4931{
4932	char	   *val;
4933	pg_locale_t mylocale;
4934
4935	switch (typid)
4936	{
4937		case CHAROID:
4938			val = (char *) palloc(2);
4939			val[0] = DatumGetChar(value);
4940			val[1] = '\0';
4941			break;
4942		case BPCHAROID:
4943		case VARCHAROID:
4944		case TEXTOID:
4945			val = TextDatumGetCString(value);
4946			break;
4947		case NAMEOID:
4948			{
4949				NameData   *nm = (NameData *) DatumGetPointer(value);
4950
4951				val = pstrdup(NameStr(*nm));
4952				break;
4953			}
4954		default:
4955			*failure = true;
4956			return NULL;
4957	}
4958
4959	mylocale = pg_newlocale_from_collation(collid);
4960
4961	if (!mylocale->collate_is_c)
4962	{
4963		char	   *xfrmstr;
4964		size_t		xfrmlen;
4965		size_t		xfrmlen2 PG_USED_FOR_ASSERTS_ONLY;
4966
4967		/*
4968		 * XXX: We could guess at a suitable output buffer size and only call
4969		 * pg_strxfrm() twice if our guess is too small.
4970		 *
4971		 * XXX: strxfrm doesn't support UTF-8 encoding on Win32, it can return
4972		 * bogus data or set an error. This is not really a problem unless it
4973		 * crashes since it will only give an estimation error and nothing
4974		 * fatal.
4975		 *
4976		 * XXX: we do not check pg_strxfrm_enabled(). On some platforms and in
4977		 * some cases, libc strxfrm() may return the wrong results, but that
4978		 * will only lead to an estimation error.
4979		 */
4980		xfrmlen = pg_strxfrm(NULL, val, 0, mylocale);
4981#ifdef WIN32
4982
4983		/*
4984		 * On Windows, strxfrm returns INT_MAX when an error occurs. Instead
4985		 * of trying to allocate this much memory (and fail), just return the
4986		 * original string unmodified as if we were in the C locale.
4987		 */
4988		if (xfrmlen == INT_MAX)
4989			return val;
4990#endif
4991		xfrmstr = (char *) palloc(xfrmlen + 1);
4992		xfrmlen2 = pg_strxfrm(xfrmstr, val, xfrmlen + 1, mylocale);
4993
4994		/*
4995		 * Some systems (e.g., glibc) can return a smaller value from the
4996		 * second call than the first; thus the Assert must be <= not ==.
4997		 */
4998		Assert(xfrmlen2 <= xfrmlen);
4999		pfree(val);
5000		val = xfrmstr;
5001	}
5002
5003	return val;
5004}
5005
5006/*
5007 * Do convert_to_scalar()'s work for any bytea data type.
5008 *
5009 * Very similar to convert_string_to_scalar except we can't assume
5010 * null-termination and therefore pass explicit lengths around.
5011 *
5012 * Also, assumptions about likely "normal" ranges of characters have been
5013 * removed - a data range of 0..255 is always used, for now.  (Perhaps
5014 * someday we will add information about actual byte data range to
5015 * pg_statistic.)
5016 */
5017static void
5018convert_bytea_to_scalar(Datum value,
5019						double *scaledvalue,
5020						Datum lobound,
5021						double *scaledlobound,
5022						Datum hibound,
5023						double *scaledhibound)
5024{
5025	bytea	   *valuep = DatumGetByteaPP(value);
5026	bytea	   *loboundp = DatumGetByteaPP(lobound);
5027	bytea	   *hiboundp = DatumGetByteaPP(hibound);
5028	int			rangelo,
5029				rangehi,
5030				valuelen = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(valuep),
5031				loboundlen = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(loboundp),
5032				hiboundlen = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(hiboundp),
5033				i,
5034				minlen;
5035	unsigned char *valstr = (unsigned char *) VARDATA_ANY(valuep);
5036	unsigned char *lostr = (unsigned char *) VARDATA_ANY(loboundp);
5037	unsigned char *histr = (unsigned char *) VARDATA_ANY(hiboundp);
5038
5039	/*
5040	 * Assume bytea data is uniformly distributed across all byte values.
5041	 */
5042	rangelo = 0;
5043	rangehi = 255;
5044
5045	/*
5046	 * Now strip any common prefix of the three strings.
5047	 */
5048	minlen = Min(Min(valuelen, loboundlen), hiboundlen);
5049	for (i = 0; i < minlen; i++)
5050	{
5051		if (*lostr != *histr || *lostr != *valstr)
5052			break;
5053		lostr++, histr++, valstr++;
5054		loboundlen--, hiboundlen--, valuelen--;
5055	}
5056
5057	/*
5058	 * Now we can do the conversions.
5059	 */
5060	*scaledvalue = convert_one_bytea_to_scalar(valstr, valuelen, rangelo, rangehi);
5061	*scaledlobound = convert_one_bytea_to_scalar(lostr, loboundlen, rangelo, rangehi);
5062	*scaledhibound = convert_one_bytea_to_scalar(histr, hiboundlen, rangelo, rangehi);
5063}
5064
5065static double
5066convert_one_bytea_to_scalar(unsigned char *value, int valuelen,
5067							int rangelo, int rangehi)
5068{
5069	double		num,
5070				denom,
5071				base;
5072
5073	if (valuelen <= 0)
5074		return 0.0;				/* empty string has scalar value 0 */
5075
5076	/*
5077	 * Since base is 256, need not consider more than about 10 chars (even
5078	 * this many seems like overkill)
5079	 */
5080	if (valuelen > 10)
5081		valuelen = 10;
5082
5083	/* Convert initial characters to fraction */
5084	base = rangehi - rangelo + 1;
5085	num = 0.0;
5086	denom = base;
5087	while (valuelen-- > 0)
5088	{
5089		int			ch = *value++;
5090
5091		if (ch < rangelo)
5092			ch = rangelo - 1;
5093		else if (ch > rangehi)
5094			ch = rangehi + 1;
5095		num += ((double) (ch - rangelo)) / denom;
5096		denom *= base;
5097	}
5098
5099	return num;
5100}
5101
5102/*
5103 * Do convert_to_scalar()'s work for any timevalue data type.
5104 *
5105 * On failure (e.g., unsupported typid), set *failure to true;
5106 * otherwise, that variable is not changed.
5107 */
5108static double
5109convert_timevalue_to_scalar(Datum value, Oid typid, bool *failure)
5110{
5111	switch (typid)
5112	{
5113		case TIMESTAMPOID:
5114			return DatumGetTimestamp(value);
5115		case TIMESTAMPTZOID:
5116			return DatumGetTimestampTz(value);
5117		case DATEOID:
5118			return date2timestamp_no_overflow(DatumGetDateADT(value));
5119		case INTERVALOID:
5120			{
5121				Interval   *interval = DatumGetIntervalP(value);
5122
5123				/*
5124				 * Convert the month part of Interval to days using assumed
5125				 * average month length of 365.25/12.0 days.  Not too
5126				 * accurate, but plenty good enough for our purposes.
5127				 *
5128				 * This also works for infinite intervals, which just have all
5129				 * fields set to INT_MIN/INT_MAX, and so will produce a result
5130				 * smaller/larger than any finite interval.
5131				 */
5132				return interval->time + interval->day * (double) USECS_PER_DAY +
5133					interval->month * ((DAYS_PER_YEAR / (double) MONTHS_PER_YEAR) * USECS_PER_DAY);
5134			}
5135		case TIMEOID:
5136			return DatumGetTimeADT(value);
5137		case TIMETZOID:
5138			{
5139				TimeTzADT  *timetz = DatumGetTimeTzADTP(value);
5140
5141				/* use GMT-equivalent time */
5142				return (double) (timetz->time + (timetz->zone * 1000000.0));
5143			}
5144	}
5145
5146	*failure = true;
5147	return 0;
5148}
5149
5150
5151/*
5152 * get_restriction_variable
5153 *		Examine the args of a restriction clause to see if it's of the
5154 *		form (variable op pseudoconstant) or (pseudoconstant op variable),
5155 *		where "variable" could be either a Var or an expression in vars of a
5156 *		single relation.  If so, extract information about the variable,
5157 *		and also indicate which side it was on and the other argument.
5158 *
5159 * Inputs:
5160 *	root: the planner info
5161 *	args: clause argument list
5162 *	varRelid: see specs for restriction selectivity functions
5163 *
5164 * Outputs: (these are valid only if true is returned)
5165 *	*vardata: gets information about variable (see examine_variable)
5166 *	*other: gets other clause argument, aggressively reduced to a constant
5167 *	*varonleft: set true if variable is on the left, false if on the right
5168 *
5169 * Returns true if a variable is identified, otherwise false.
5170 *
5171 * Note: if there are Vars on both sides of the clause, we must fail, because
5172 * callers are expecting that the other side will act like a pseudoconstant.
5173 */
5174bool
5175get_restriction_variable(PlannerInfo *root, List *args, int varRelid,
5176						 VariableStatData *vardata, Node **other,
5177						 bool *varonleft)
5178{
5179	Node	   *left,
5180			   *right;
5181	VariableStatData rdata;
5182
5183	/* Fail if not a binary opclause (probably shouldn't happen) */
5184	if (list_length(args) != 2)
5185		return false;
5186
5187	left = (Node *) linitial(args);
5188	right = (Node *) lsecond(args);
5189
5190	/*
5191	 * Examine both sides.  Note that when varRelid is nonzero, Vars of other
5192	 * relations will be treated as pseudoconstants.
5193	 */
5194	examine_variable(root, left, varRelid, vardata);
5195	examine_variable(root, right, varRelid, &rdata);
5196
5197	/*
5198	 * If one side is a variable and the other not, we win.
5199	 */
5200	if (vardata->rel && rdata.rel == NULL)
5201	{
5202		*varonleft = true;
5203		*other = estimate_expression_value(root, rdata.var);
5204		/* Assume we need no ReleaseVariableStats(rdata) here */
5205		return true;
5206	}
5207
5208	if (vardata->rel == NULL && rdata.rel)
5209	{
5210		*varonleft = false;
5211		*other = estimate_expression_value(root, vardata->var);
5212		/* Assume we need no ReleaseVariableStats(*vardata) here */
5213		*vardata = rdata;
5214		return true;
5215	}
5216
5217	/* Oops, clause has wrong structure (probably var op var) */
5218	ReleaseVariableStats(*vardata);
5219	ReleaseVariableStats(rdata);
5220
5221	return false;
5222}
5223
5224/*
5225 * get_join_variables
5226 *		Apply examine_variable() to each side of a join clause.
5227 *		Also, attempt to identify whether the join clause has the same
5228 *		or reversed sense compared to the SpecialJoinInfo.
5229 *
5230 * We consider the join clause "normal" if it is "lhs_var OP rhs_var",
5231 * or "reversed" if it is "rhs_var OP lhs_var".  In complicated cases
5232 * where we can't tell for sure, we default to assuming it's normal.
5233 */
5234void
5235get_join_variables(PlannerInfo *root, List *args, SpecialJoinInfo *sjinfo,
5236				   VariableStatData *vardata1, VariableStatData *vardata2,
5237				   bool *join_is_reversed)
5238{
5239	Node	   *left,
5240			   *right;
5241
5242	if (list_length(args) != 2)
5243		elog(ERROR, "join operator should take two arguments");
5244
5245	left = (Node *) linitial(args);
5246	right = (Node *) lsecond(args);
5247
5248	examine_variable(root, left, 0, vardata1);
5249	examine_variable(root, right, 0, vardata2);
5250
5251	if (vardata1->rel &&
5252		bms_is_subset(vardata1->rel->relids, sjinfo->syn_righthand))
5253		*join_is_reversed = true;	/* var1 is on RHS */
5254	else if (vardata2->rel &&
5255			 bms_is_subset(vardata2->rel->relids, sjinfo->syn_lefthand))
5256		*join_is_reversed = true;	/* var2 is on LHS */
5257	else
5258		*join_is_reversed = false;
5259}
5260
5261/* statext_expressions_load copies the tuple, so just pfree it. */
5262static void
5263ReleaseDummy(HeapTuple tuple)
5264{
5265	pfree(tuple);
5266}
5267
5268/*
5269 * examine_variable
5270 *		Try to look up statistical data about an expression.
5271 *		Fill in a VariableStatData struct to describe the expression.
5272 *
5273 * Inputs:
5274 *	root: the planner info
5275 *	node: the expression tree to examine
5276 *	varRelid: see specs for restriction selectivity functions
5277 *
5278 * Outputs: *vardata is filled as follows:
5279 *	var: the input expression (with any phvs or binary relabeling stripped,
5280 *		if it is or contains a variable; but otherwise unchanged)
5281 *	rel: RelOptInfo for relation containing variable; NULL if expression
5282 *		contains no Vars (NOTE this could point to a RelOptInfo of a
5283 *		subquery, not one in the current query).
5284 *	statsTuple: the pg_statistic entry for the variable, if one exists;
5285 *		otherwise NULL.
5286 *	freefunc: pointer to a function to release statsTuple with.
5287 *	vartype: exposed type of the expression; this should always match
5288 *		the declared input type of the operator we are estimating for.
5289 *	atttype, atttypmod: actual type/typmod of the "var" expression.  This is
5290 *		commonly the same as the exposed type of the variable argument,
5291 *		but can be different in binary-compatible-type cases.
5292 *	isunique: true if we were able to match the var to a unique index, a
5293 *		single-column DISTINCT or GROUP-BY clause, implying its values are
5294 *		unique for this query.  (Caution: this should be trusted for
5295 *		statistical purposes only, since we do not check indimmediate nor
5296 *		verify that the exact same definition of equality applies.)
5297 *	acl_ok: true if current user has permission to read all table rows from
5298 *		the column(s) underlying the pg_statistic entry.  This is consulted by
5299 *		statistic_proc_security_check().
5300 *
5301 * Caller is responsible for doing ReleaseVariableStats() before exiting.
5302 */
5303void
5304examine_variable(PlannerInfo *root, Node *node, int varRelid,
5305				 VariableStatData *vardata)
5306{
5307	Node	   *basenode;
5308	Relids		varnos;
5309	Relids		basevarnos;
5310	RelOptInfo *onerel;
5311
5312	/* Make sure we don't return dangling pointers in vardata */
5313	MemSet(vardata, 0, sizeof(VariableStatData));
5314
5315	/* Save the exposed type of the expression */
5316	vardata->vartype = exprType(node);
5317
5318	/*
5319	 * PlaceHolderVars are transparent for the purpose of statistics lookup;
5320	 * they do not alter the value distribution of the underlying expression.
5321	 * However, they can obscure the structure, preventing us from recognizing
5322	 * matches to base columns, index expressions, or extended statistics.  So
5323	 * strip them out first.
5324	 */
5325	basenode = strip_all_phvs_deep(root, node);
5326
5327	/*
5328	 * Look inside any binary-compatible relabeling.  We need to handle nested
5329	 * RelabelType nodes here, because the prior stripping of PlaceHolderVars
5330	 * may have brought separate RelabelTypes into adjacency.
5331	 */
5332	while (IsA(basenode, RelabelType))
5333		basenode = (Node *) ((RelabelType *) basenode)->arg;
5334
5335	/* Fast path for a simple Var */
5336	if (IsA(basenode, Var) &&
5337		(varRelid == 0 || varRelid == ((Var *) basenode)->varno))
5338	{
5339		Var		   *var = (Var *) basenode;
5340
5341		/* Set up result fields other than the stats tuple */
5342		vardata->var = basenode;	/* return Var without phvs or relabeling */
5343		vardata->rel = find_base_rel(root, var->varno);
5344		vardata->atttype = var->vartype;
5345		vardata->atttypmod = var->vartypmod;
5346		vardata->isunique = has_unique_index(vardata->rel, var->varattno);
5347
5348		/* Try to locate some stats */
5349		examine_simple_variable(root, var, vardata);
5350
5351		return;
5352	}
5353
5354	/*
5355	 * Okay, it's a more complicated expression.  Determine variable
5356	 * membership.  Note that when varRelid isn't zero, only vars of that
5357	 * relation are considered "real" vars.
5358	 */
5359	varnos = pull_varnos(root, basenode);
5360	basevarnos = bms_difference(varnos, root->outer_join_rels);
5361
5362	onerel = NULL;
5363
5364	if (bms_is_empty(basevarnos))
5365	{
5366		/* No Vars at all ... must be pseudo-constant clause */
5367	}
5368	else
5369	{
5370		int			relid;
5371
5372		/* Check if the expression is in vars of a single base relation */
5373		if (bms_get_singleton_member(basevarnos, &relid))
5374		{
5375			if (varRelid == 0 || varRelid == relid)
5376			{
5377				onerel = find_base_rel(root, relid);
5378				vardata->rel = onerel;
5379				node = basenode;	/* strip any phvs or relabeling */
5380			}
5381			/* else treat it as a constant */
5382		}
5383		else
5384		{
5385			/* varnos has multiple relids */
5386			if (varRelid == 0)
5387			{
5388				/* treat it as a variable of a join relation */
5389				vardata->rel = find_join_rel(root, varnos);
5390				node = basenode;	/* strip any phvs or relabeling */
5391			}
5392			else if (bms_is_member(varRelid, varnos))
5393			{
5394				/* ignore the vars belonging to other relations */
5395				vardata->rel = find_base_rel(root, varRelid);
5396				node = basenode;	/* strip any phvs or relabeling */
5397				/* note: no point in expressional-index search here */
5398			}
5399			/* else treat it as a constant */
5400		}
5401	}
5402
5403	bms_free(basevarnos);
5404
5405	vardata->var = node;
5406	vardata->atttype = exprType(node);
5407	vardata->atttypmod = exprTypmod(node);
5408
5409	if (onerel)
5410	{
5411		/*
5412		 * We have an expression in vars of a single relation.  Try to match
5413		 * it to expressional index columns, in hopes of finding some
5414		 * statistics.
5415		 *
5416		 * Note that we consider all index columns including INCLUDE columns,
5417		 * since there could be stats for such columns.  But the test for
5418		 * uniqueness needs to be warier.
5419		 *
5420		 * XXX it's conceivable that there are multiple matches with different
5421		 * index opfamilies; if so, we need to pick one that matches the
5422		 * operator we are estimating for.  FIXME later.
5423		 */
5424		ListCell   *ilist;
5425		ListCell   *slist;
5426
5427		/*
5428		 * The nullingrels bits within the expression could prevent us from
5429		 * matching it to expressional index columns or to the expressions in
5430		 * extended statistics.  So strip them out first.
5431		 */
5432		if (bms_overlap(varnos, root->outer_join_rels))
5433			node = remove_nulling_relids(node, root->outer_join_rels, NULL);
5434
5435		foreach(ilist, onerel->indexlist)
5436		{
5437			IndexOptInfo *index = (IndexOptInfo *) lfirst(ilist);
5438			ListCell   *indexpr_item;
5439			int			pos;
5440
5441			indexpr_item = list_head(index->indexprs);
5442			if (indexpr_item == NULL)
5443				continue;		/* no expressions here... */
5444
5445			for (pos = 0; pos < index->ncolumns; pos++)
5446			{
5447				if (index->indexkeys[pos] == 0)
5448				{
5449					Node	   *indexkey;
5450
5451					if (indexpr_item == NULL)
5452						elog(ERROR, "too few entries in indexprs list");
5453					indexkey = (Node *) lfirst(indexpr_item);
5454					if (indexkey && IsA(indexkey, RelabelType))
5455						indexkey = (Node *) ((RelabelType *) indexkey)->arg;
5456					if (equal(node, indexkey))
5457					{
5458						/*
5459						 * Found a match ... is it a unique index? Tests here
5460						 * should match has_unique_index().
5461						 */
5462						if (index->unique &&
5463							index->nkeycolumns == 1 &&
5464							pos == 0 &&
5465							(index->indpred == NIL || index->predOK))
5466							vardata->isunique = true;
5467
5468						/*
5469						 * Has it got stats?  We only consider stats for
5470						 * non-partial indexes, since partial indexes probably
5471						 * don't reflect whole-relation statistics; the above
5472						 * check for uniqueness is the only info we take from
5473						 * a partial index.
5474						 *
5475						 * An index stats hook, however, must make its own
5476						 * decisions about what to do with partial indexes.
5477						 */
5478						if (get_index_stats_hook &&
5479							(*get_index_stats_hook) (root, index->indexoid,
5480													 pos + 1, vardata))
5481						{
5482							/*
5483							 * The hook took control of acquiring a stats
5484							 * tuple.  If it did supply a tuple, it'd better
5485							 * have supplied a freefunc.
5486							 */
5487							if (HeapTupleIsValid(vardata->statsTuple) &&
5488								!vardata->freefunc)
5489								elog(ERROR, "no function provided to release variable stats with");
5490						}
5491						else if (index->indpred == NIL)
5492						{
5493							vardata->statsTuple =
5494								SearchSysCache3(STATRELATTINH,
5495												ObjectIdGetDatum(index->indexoid),
5496												Int16GetDatum(pos + 1),
5497												BoolGetDatum(false));
5498							vardata->freefunc = ReleaseSysCache;
5499
5500							if (HeapTupleIsValid(vardata->statsTuple))
5501							{
5502								/*
5503								 * Test if user has permission to access all
5504								 * rows from the index's table.
5505								 *
5506								 * For simplicity, we insist on the whole
5507								 * table being selectable, rather than trying
5508								 * to identify which column(s) the index
5509								 * depends on.
5510								 *
5511								 * Note that for an inheritance child,
5512								 * permissions are checked on the inheritance
5513								 * root parent, and whole-table select
5514								 * privilege on the parent doesn't quite
5515								 * guarantee that the user could read all
5516								 * columns of the child.  But in practice it's
5517								 * unlikely that any interesting security
5518								 * violation could result from allowing access
5519								 * to the expression index's stats, so we
5520								 * allow it anyway.  See similar code in
5521								 * examine_simple_variable() for additional
5522								 * comments.
5523								 */
5524								vardata->acl_ok =
5525									all_rows_selectable(root,
5526														index->rel->relid,
5527														NULL);
5528							}
5529							else
5530							{
5531								/* suppress leakproofness checks later */
5532								vardata->acl_ok = true;
5533							}
5534						}
5535						if (vardata->statsTuple)
5536							break;
5537					}
5538					indexpr_item = lnext(index->indexprs, indexpr_item);
5539				}
5540			}
5541			if (vardata->statsTuple)
5542				break;
5543		}
5544
5545		/*
5546		 * Search extended statistics for one with a matching expression.
5547		 * There might be multiple ones, so just grab the first one. In the
5548		 * future, we might consider the statistics target (and pick the most
5549		 * accurate statistics) and maybe some other parameters.
5550		 */
5551		foreach(slist, onerel->statlist)
5552		{
5553			StatisticExtInfo *info = (StatisticExtInfo *) lfirst(slist);
5554			RangeTblEntry *rte = planner_rt_fetch(onerel->relid, root);
5555			ListCell   *expr_item;
5556			int			pos;
5557
5558			/*
5559			 * Stop once we've found statistics for the expression (either
5560			 * from extended stats, or for an index in the preceding loop).
5561			 */
5562			if (vardata->statsTuple)
5563				break;
5564
5565			/* skip stats without per-expression stats */
5566			if (info->kind != STATS_EXT_EXPRESSIONS)
5567				continue;
5568
5569			/* skip stats with mismatching stxdinherit value */
5570			if (info->inherit != rte->inh)
5571				continue;
5572
5573			pos = 0;
5574			foreach(expr_item, info->exprs)
5575			{
5576				Node	   *expr = (Node *) lfirst(expr_item);
5577
5578				Assert(expr);
5579
5580				/* strip RelabelType before comparing it */
5581				if (expr && IsA(expr, RelabelType))
5582					expr = (Node *) ((RelabelType *) expr)->arg;
5583
5584				/* found a match, see if we can extract pg_statistic row */
5585				if (equal(node, expr))
5586				{
5587					/*
5588					 * XXX Not sure if we should cache the tuple somewhere.
5589					 * Now we just create a new copy every time.
5590					 */
5591					vardata->statsTuple =
5592						statext_expressions_load(info->statOid, rte->inh, pos);
5593
5594					/* Nothing to release if no data found */
5595					if (vardata->statsTuple != NULL)
5596					{
5597						vardata->freefunc = ReleaseDummy;
5598					}
5599
5600					/*
5601					 * Test if user has permission to access all rows from the
5602					 * table.
5603					 *
5604					 * For simplicity, we insist on the whole table being
5605					 * selectable, rather than trying to identify which
5606					 * column(s) the statistics object depends on.
5607					 *
5608					 * Note that for an inheritance child, permissions are
5609					 * checked on the inheritance root parent, and whole-table
5610					 * select privilege on the parent doesn't quite guarantee
5611					 * that the user could read all columns of the child.  But
5612					 * in practice it's unlikely that any interesting security
5613					 * violation could result from allowing access to the
5614					 * expression stats, so we allow it anyway.  See similar
5615					 * code in examine_simple_variable() for additional
5616					 * comments.
5617					 */
5618					vardata->acl_ok = all_rows_selectable(root,
5619														  onerel->relid,
5620														  NULL);
5621
5622					break;
5623				}
5624
5625				pos++;
5626			}
5627		}
5628	}
5629
5630	bms_free(varnos);
5631}
5632
5633/*
5634 * strip_all_phvs_deep
5635 *		Deeply strip all PlaceHolderVars in an expression.
5636
5637 * As a performance optimization, we first use a lightweight walker to check
5638 * for the presence of any PlaceHolderVars.  The expensive mutator is invoked
5639 * only if a PlaceHolderVar is found, avoiding unnecessary memory allocation
5640 * and tree copying in the common case where no PlaceHolderVars are present.
5641 */
5642static Node *
5643strip_all_phvs_deep(PlannerInfo *root, Node *node)
5644{
5645	/* If there are no PHVs anywhere, we needn't work hard */
5646	if (root->glob->lastPHId == 0)
5647		return node;
5648
5649	if (!contain_placeholder_walker(node, NULL))
5650		return node;
5651	return strip_all_phvs_mutator(node, NULL);
5652}
5653
5654/*
5655 * contain_placeholder_walker
5656 *		Lightweight walker to check if an expression contains any
5657 *		PlaceHolderVars
5658 */
5659static bool
5660contain_placeholder_walker(Node *node, void *context)
5661{
5662	if (node == NULL)
5663		return false;
5664	if (IsA(node, PlaceHolderVar))
5665		return true;
5666
5667	return expression_tree_walker(node, contain_placeholder_walker, context);
5668}
5669
5670/*
5671 * strip_all_phvs_mutator
5672 *		Mutator to deeply strip all PlaceHolderVars
5673 */
5674static Node *
5675strip_all_phvs_mutator(Node *node, void *context)
5676{
5677	if (node == NULL)
5678		return NULL;
5679	if (IsA(node, PlaceHolderVar))
5680	{
5681		/* Strip it and recurse into its contained expression */
5682		PlaceHolderVar *phv = (PlaceHolderVar *) node;
5683
5684		return strip_all_phvs_mutator((Node *) phv->phexpr, context);
5685	}
5686
5687	return expression_tree_mutator(node, strip_all_phvs_mutator, context);
5688}
5689
5690/*
5691 * examine_simple_variable
5692 *		Handle a simple Var for examine_variable
5693 *
5694 * This is split out as a subroutine so that we can recurse to deal with
5695 * Vars referencing subqueries (either sub-SELECT-in-FROM or CTE style).
5696 *
5697 * We already filled in all the fields of *vardata except for the stats tuple.
5698 */
5699static void
5700examine_simple_variable(PlannerInfo *root, Var *var,
5701						VariableStatData *vardata)
5702{
5703	RangeTblEntry *rte = root->simple_rte_array[var->varno];
5704
5705	Assert(IsA(rte, RangeTblEntry));
5706
5707	if (get_relation_stats_hook &&
5708		(*get_relation_stats_hook) (root, rte, var->varattno, vardata))
5709	{
5710		/*
5711		 * The hook took control of acquiring a stats tuple.  If it did supply
5712		 * a tuple, it'd better have supplied a freefunc.
5713		 */
5714		if (HeapTupleIsValid(vardata->statsTuple) &&
5715			!vardata->freefunc)
5716			elog(ERROR, "no function provided to release variable stats with");
5717	}
5718	else if (rte->rtekind == RTE_RELATION)
5719	{
5720		/*
5721		 * Plain table or parent of an inheritance appendrel, so look up the
5722		 * column in pg_statistic
5723		 */
5724		vardata->statsTuple = SearchSysCache3(STATRELATTINH,
5725											  ObjectIdGetDatum(rte->relid),
5726											  Int16GetDatum(var->varattno),
5727											  BoolGetDatum(rte->inh));
5728		vardata->freefunc = ReleaseSysCache;
5729
5730		if (HeapTupleIsValid(vardata->statsTuple))
5731		{
5732			/*
5733			 * Test if user has permission to read all rows from this column.
5734			 *
5735			 * This requires that the user has the appropriate SELECT
5736			 * privileges and that there are no securityQuals from security
5737			 * barrier views or RLS policies.  If that's not the case, then we
5738			 * only permit leakproof functions to be passed pg_statistic data
5739			 * in vardata, otherwise the functions might reveal data that the
5740			 * user doesn't have permission to see --- see
5741			 * statistic_proc_security_check().
5742			 */
5743			vardata->acl_ok =
5744				all_rows_selectable(root, var->varno,
5745									bms_make_singleton(var->varattno - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber));
5746		}
5747		else
5748		{
5749			/* suppress any possible leakproofness checks later */
5750			vardata->acl_ok = true;
5751		}
5752	}
5753	else if ((rte->rtekind == RTE_SUBQUERY && !rte->inh) ||
5754			 (rte->rtekind == RTE_CTE && !rte->self_reference))
5755	{
5756		/*
5757		 * Plain subquery (not one that was converted to an appendrel) or
5758		 * non-recursive CTE.  In either case, we can try to find out what the
5759		 * Var refers to within the subquery.  We skip this for appendrel and
5760		 * recursive-CTE cases because any column stats we did find would
5761		 * likely not be very relevant.
5762		 */
5763		PlannerInfo *subroot;
5764		Query	   *subquery;
5765		List	   *subtlist;
5766		TargetEntry *ste;
5767
5768		/*
5769		 * Punt if it's a whole-row var rather than a plain column reference.
5770		 */
5771		if (var->varattno == InvalidAttrNumber)
5772			return;
5773
5774		/*
5775		 * Otherwise, find the subquery's planner subroot.
5776		 */
5777		if (rte->rtekind == RTE_SUBQUERY)
5778		{
5779			RelOptInfo *rel;
5780
5781			/*
5782			 * Fetch RelOptInfo for subquery.  Note that we don't change the
5783			 * rel returned in vardata, since caller expects it to be a rel of
5784			 * the caller's query level.  Because we might already be
5785			 * recursing, we can't use that rel pointer either, but have to
5786			 * look up the Var's rel afresh.
5787			 */
5788			rel = find_base_rel(root, var->varno);
5789
5790			subroot = rel->subroot;
5791		}
5792		else
5793		{
5794			/* CTE case is more difficult */
5795			PlannerInfo *cteroot;
5796			Index		levelsup;
5797			int			ndx;
5798			int			plan_id;
5799			ListCell   *lc;
5800
5801			/*
5802			 * Find the referenced CTE, and locate the subroot previously made
5803			 * for it.
5804			 */
5805			levelsup = rte->ctelevelsup;
5806			cteroot = root;
5807			while (levelsup-- > 0)
5808			{
5809				cteroot = cteroot->parent_root;
5810				if (!cteroot)	/* shouldn't happen */
5811					elog(ERROR, "bad levelsup for CTE \"%s\"", rte->ctename);
5812			}
5813
5814			/*
5815			 * Note: cte_plan_ids can be shorter than cteList, if we are still
5816			 * working on planning the CTEs (ie, this is a side-reference from
5817			 * another CTE).  So we mustn't use forboth here.
5818			 */
5819			ndx = 0;
5820			foreach(lc, cteroot->parse->cteList)
5821			{
5822				CommonTableExpr *cte = (CommonTableExpr *) lfirst(lc);
5823
5824				if (strcmp(cte->ctename, rte->ctename) == 0)
5825					break;
5826				ndx++;
5827			}
5828			if (lc == NULL)		/* shouldn't happen */
5829				elog(ERROR, "could not find CTE \"%s\"", rte->ctename);
5830			if (ndx >= list_length(cteroot->cte_plan_ids))
5831				elog(ERROR, "could not find plan for CTE \"%s\"", rte->ctename);
5832			plan_id = list_nth_int(cteroot->cte_plan_ids, ndx);
5833			if (plan_id <= 0)
5834				elog(ERROR, "no plan was made for CTE \"%s\"", rte->ctename);
5835			subroot = list_nth(root->glob->subroots, plan_id - 1);
5836		}
5837
5838		/* If the subquery hasn't been planned yet, we have to punt */
5839		if (subroot == NULL)
5840			return;
5841		Assert(IsA(subroot, PlannerInfo));
5842
5843		/*
5844		 * We must use the subquery parsetree as mangled by the planner, not
5845		 * the raw version from the RTE, because we need a Var that will refer
5846		 * to the subroot's live RelOptInfos.  For instance, if any subquery
5847		 * pullup happened during planning, Vars in the targetlist might have
5848		 * gotten replaced, and we need to see the replacement expressions.
5849		 */
5850		subquery = subroot->parse;
5851		Assert(IsA(subquery, Query));
5852
5853		/*
5854		 * Punt if subquery uses set operations or grouping sets, as these
5855		 * will mash underlying columns' stats beyond recognition.  (Set ops
5856		 * are particularly nasty; if we forged ahead, we would return stats
5857		 * relevant to only the leftmost subselect...)	DISTINCT is also
5858		 * problematic, but we check that later because there is a possibility
5859		 * of learning something even with it.
5860		 */
5861		if (subquery->setOperations ||
5862			subquery->groupingSets)
5863			return;
5864
5865		/* Get the subquery output expression referenced by the upper Var */
5866		if (subquery->returningList)
5867			subtlist = subquery->returningList;
5868		else
5869			subtlist = subquery->targetList;
5870		ste = get_tle_by_resno(subtlist, var->varattno);
5871		if (ste == NULL || ste->resjunk)
5872			elog(ERROR, "subquery %s does not have attribute %d",
5873				 rte->eref->aliasname, var->varattno);
5874		var = (Var *) ste->expr;
5875
5876		/*
5877		 * If subquery uses DISTINCT, we can't make use of any stats for the
5878		 * variable ... but, if it's the only DISTINCT column, we are entitled
5879		 * to consider it unique.  We do the test this way so that it works
5880		 * for cases involving DISTINCT ON.
5881		 */
5882		if (subquery->distinctClause)
5883		{
5884			if (list_length(subquery->distinctClause) == 1 &&
5885				targetIsInSortList(ste, InvalidOid, subquery->distinctClause))
5886				vardata->isunique = true;
5887			/* cannot go further */
5888			return;
5889		}
5890
5891		/* The same idea as with DISTINCT clause works for a GROUP-BY too */
5892		if (subquery->groupClause)
5893		{
5894			if (list_length(subquery->groupClause) == 1 &&
5895				targetIsInSortList(ste, InvalidOid, subquery->groupClause))
5896				vardata->isunique = true;
5897			/* cannot go further */
5898			return;
5899		}
5900
5901		/*
5902		 * If the sub-query originated from a view with the security_barrier
5903		 * attribute, we must not look at the variable's statistics, though it
5904		 * seems all right to notice the existence of a DISTINCT clause. So
5905		 * stop here.
5906		 *
5907		 * This is probably a harsher restriction than necessary; it's
5908		 * certainly OK for the selectivity estimator (which is a C function,
5909		 * and therefore omnipotent anyway) to look at the statistics.  But
5910		 * many selectivity estimators will happily *invoke the operator
5911		 * function* to try to work out a good estimate - and that's not OK.
5912		 * So for now, don't dig down for stats.
5913		 */
5914		if (rte->security_barrier)
5915			return;
5916
5917		/* Can only handle a simple Var of subquery's query level */
5918		if (var && IsA(var, Var) &&
5919			var->varlevelsup == 0)
5920		{
5921			/*
5922			 * OK, recurse into the subquery.  Note that the original setting
5923			 * of vardata->isunique (which will surely be false) is left
5924			 * unchanged in this situation.  That's what we want, since even
5925			 * if the underlying column is unique, the subquery may have
5926			 * joined to other tables in a way that creates duplicates.
5927			 */
5928			examine_simple_variable(subroot, var, vardata);
5929		}
5930	}
5931	else
5932	{
5933		/*
5934		 * Otherwise, the Var comes from a FUNCTION or VALUES RTE.  (We won't
5935		 * see RTE_JOIN here because join alias Vars have already been
5936		 * flattened.)	There's not much we can do with function outputs, but
5937		 * maybe someday try to be smarter about VALUES.
5938		 */
5939	}
5940}
5941
5942/*
5943 * all_rows_selectable
5944 *		Test whether the user has permission to select all rows from a given
5945 *		relation.
5946 *
5947 * Inputs:
5948 *	root: the planner info
5949 *	varno: the index of the relation (assumed to be an RTE_RELATION)
5950 *	varattnos: the attributes for which permission is required, or NULL if
5951 *		whole-table access is required
5952 *
5953 * Returns true if the user has the required select permissions, and there are
5954 * no securityQuals from security barrier views or RLS policies.
5955 *
5956 * Note that if the relation is an inheritance child relation, securityQuals
5957 * and access permissions are checked against the inheritance root parent (the
5958 * relation actually mentioned in the query) --- see the comments in
5959 * expand_single_inheritance_child() for an explanation of why it has to be
5960 * done this way.
5961 *
5962 * If varattnos is non-NULL, its attribute numbers should be offset by
5963 * FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber so that system attributes can be
5964 * checked.  If varattnos is NULL, only table-level SELECT privileges are
5965 * checked, not any column-level privileges.
5966 *
5967 * Note: if the relation is accessed via a view, this function actually tests
5968 * whether the view owner has permission to select from the relation.  To
5969 * ensure that the current user has permission, it is also necessary to check
5970 * that the current user has permission to select from the view, which we do
5971 * at planner-startup --- see subquery_planner().
5972 *
5973 * This is exported so that other estimation functions can use it.
5974 */
5975bool
5976all_rows_selectable(PlannerInfo *root, Index varno, Bitmapset *varattnos)
5977{
5978	RelOptInfo *rel = find_base_rel_noerr(root, varno);
5979	RangeTblEntry *rte = planner_rt_fetch(varno, root);
5980	Oid			userid;
5981	int			varattno;
5982
5983	Assert(rte->rtekind == RTE_RELATION);
5984
5985	/*
5986	 * Determine the user ID to use for privilege checks (either the current
5987	 * user or the view owner, if we're accessing the table via a view).
5988	 *
5989	 * Normally the relation will have an associated RelOptInfo from which we
5990	 * can find the userid, but it might not if it's a RETURNING Var for an
5991	 * INSERT target relation.  In that case use the RTEPermissionInfo
5992	 * associated with the RTE.
5993	 *
5994	 * If we navigate up to a parent relation, we keep using the same userid,
5995	 * since it's the same in all relations of a given inheritance tree.
5996	 */
5997	if (rel)
5998		userid = rel->userid;
5999	else
6000	{
6001		RTEPermissionInfo *perminfo;
6002
6003		perminfo = getRTEPermissionInfo(root->parse->rteperminfos, rte);
6004		userid = perminfo->checkAsUser;
6005	}
6006	if (!OidIsValid(userid))
6007		userid = GetUserId();
6008
6009	/*
6010	 * Permissions and securityQuals must be checked on the table actually
6011	 * mentioned in the query, so if this is an inheritance child, navigate up
6012	 * to the inheritance root parent.  If the user can read the whole table
6013	 * or the required columns there, then they can read from the child table
6014	 * too.  For per-column checks, we must find out which of the root
6015	 * parent's attributes the child relation's attributes correspond to.
6016	 */
6017	if (root->append_rel_array != NULL)
6018	{
6019		AppendRelInfo *appinfo;
6020
6021		appinfo = root->append_rel_array[varno];
6022
6023		/*
6024		 * Partitions are mapped to their immediate parent, not the root
6025		 * parent, so must be ready to walk up multiple AppendRelInfos.  But
6026		 * stop if we hit a parent that is not RTE_RELATION --- that's a
6027		 * flattened UNION ALL subquery, not an inheritance parent.
6028		 */
6029		while (appinfo &&
6030			   planner_rt_fetch(appinfo->parent_relid,
6031								root)->rtekind == RTE_RELATION)
6032		{
6033			Bitmapset  *parent_varattnos = NULL;
6034
6035			/*
6036			 * For each child attribute, find the corresponding parent
6037			 * attribute.  In rare cases, the attribute may be local to the
6038			 * child table, in which case, we've got to live with having no
6039			 * access to this column.
6040			 */
6041			varattno = -1;
6042			while ((varattno = bms_next_member(varattnos, varattno)) >= 0)
6043			{
6044				AttrNumber	attno;
6045				AttrNumber	parent_attno;
6046
6047				attno = varattno + FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber;
6048
6049				if (attno == InvalidAttrNumber)
6050				{
6051					/*
6052					 * Whole-row reference, so must map each column of the
6053					 * child to the parent table.
6054					 */
6055					for (attno = 1; attno <= appinfo->num_child_cols; attno++)
6056					{
6057						parent_attno = appinfo->parent_colnos[attno - 1];
6058						if (parent_attno == 0)
6059							return false;	/* attr is local to child */
6060						parent_varattnos =
6061							bms_add_member(parent_varattnos,
6062										   parent_attno - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber);
6063					}
6064				}
6065				else
6066				{
6067					if (attno < 0)
6068					{
6069						/* System attnos are the same in all tables */
6070						parent_attno = attno;
6071					}
6072					else
6073					{
6074						if (attno > appinfo->num_child_cols)
6075							return false;	/* safety check */
6076						parent_attno = appinfo->parent_colnos[attno - 1];
6077						if (parent_attno == 0)
6078							return false;	/* attr is local to child */
6079					}
6080					parent_varattnos =
6081						bms_add_member(parent_varattnos,
6082									   parent_attno - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber);
6083				}
6084			}
6085
6086			/* If the parent is itself a child, continue up */
6087			varno = appinfo->parent_relid;
6088			varattnos = parent_varattnos;
6089			appinfo = root->append_rel_array[varno];
6090		}
6091
6092		/* Perform the access check on this parent rel */
6093		rte = planner_rt_fetch(varno, root);
6094		Assert(rte->rtekind == RTE_RELATION);
6095	}
6096
6097	/*
6098	 * For all rows to be accessible, there must be no securityQuals from
6099	 * security barrier views or RLS policies.
6100	 */
6101	if (rte->securityQuals != NIL)
6102		return false;
6103
6104	/*
6105	 * Test for table-level SELECT privilege.
6106	 *
6107	 * If varattnos is non-NULL, this is sufficient to give access to all
6108	 * requested attributes, even for a child table, since we have verified
6109	 * that all required child columns have matching parent columns.
6110	 *
6111	 * If varattnos is NULL (whole-table access requested), this doesn't
6112	 * necessarily guarantee that the user can read all columns of a child
6113	 * table, but we allow it anyway (see comments in examine_variable()) and
6114	 * don't bother checking any column privileges.
6115	 */
6116	if (pg_class_aclcheck(rte->relid, userid, ACL_SELECT) == ACLCHECK_OK)
6117		return true;
6118
6119	if (varattnos == NULL)
6120		return false;			/* whole-table access requested */
6121
6122	/*
6123	 * Don't have table-level SELECT privilege, so check per-column
6124	 * privileges.
6125	 */
6126	varattno = -1;
6127	while ((varattno = bms_next_member(varattnos, varattno)) >= 0)
6128	{
6129		AttrNumber	attno = varattno + FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber;
6130
6131		if (attno == InvalidAttrNumber)
6132		{
6133			/* Whole-row reference, so must have access to all columns */
6134			if (pg_attribute_aclcheck_all(rte->relid, userid, ACL_SELECT,
6135										  ACLMASK_ALL) != ACLCHECK_OK)
6136				return false;
6137		}
6138		else
6139		{
6140			if (pg_attribute_aclcheck(rte->relid, attno, userid,
6141									  ACL_SELECT) != ACLCHECK_OK)
6142				return false;
6143		}
6144	}
6145
6146	/* If we reach here, have all required column privileges */
6147	return true;
6148}
6149
6150/*
6151 * examine_indexcol_variable
6152 *		Try to look up statistical data about an index column/expression.
6153 *		Fill in a VariableStatData struct to describe the column.
6154 *
6155 * Inputs:
6156 *	root: the planner info
6157 *	index: the index whose column we're interested in
6158 *	indexcol: 0-based index column number (subscripts index->indexkeys[])
6159 *
6160 * Outputs: *vardata is filled as follows:
6161 *	var: the input expression (with any binary relabeling stripped, if
6162 *		it is or contains a variable; but otherwise the type is preserved)
6163 *	rel: RelOptInfo for table relation containing variable.
6164 *	statsTuple: the pg_statistic entry for the variable, if one exists;
6165 *		otherwise NULL.
6166 *	freefunc: pointer to a function to release statsTuple with.
6167 *
6168 * Caller is responsible for doing ReleaseVariableStats() before exiting.
6169 */
6170static void
6171examine_indexcol_variable(PlannerInfo *root, IndexOptInfo *index,
6172						  int indexcol, VariableStatData *vardata)
6173{
6174	AttrNumber	colnum;
6175	Oid			relid;
6176
6177	if (index->indexkeys[indexcol] != 0)
6178	{
6179		/* Simple variable --- look to stats for the underlying table */
6180		RangeTblEntry *rte = planner_rt_fetch(index->rel->relid, root);
6181
6182		Assert(rte->rtekind == RTE_RELATION);
6183		relid = rte->relid;
6184		Assert(relid != InvalidOid);
6185		colnum = index->indexkeys[indexcol];
6186		vardata->rel = index->rel;
6187
6188		if (get_relation_stats_hook &&
6189			(*get_relation_stats_hook) (root, rte, colnum, vardata))
6190		{
6191			/*
6192			 * The hook took control of acquiring a stats tuple.  If it did
6193			 * supply a tuple, it'd better have supplied a freefunc.
6194			 */
6195			if (HeapTupleIsValid(vardata->statsTuple) &&
6196				!vardata->freefunc)
6197				elog(ERROR, "no function provided to release variable stats with");
6198		}
6199		else
6200		{
6201			vardata->statsTuple = SearchSysCache3(STATRELATTINH,
6202												  ObjectIdGetDatum(relid),
6203												  Int16GetDatum(colnum),
6204												  BoolGetDatum(rte->inh));
6205			vardata->freefunc = ReleaseSysCache;
6206		}
6207	}
6208	else
6209	{
6210		/* Expression --- maybe there are stats for the index itself */
6211		relid = index->indexoid;
6212		colnum = indexcol + 1;
6213
6214		if (get_index_stats_hook &&
6215			(*get_index_stats_hook) (root, relid, colnum, vardata))
6216		{
6217			/*
6218			 * The hook took control of acquiring a stats tuple.  If it did
6219			 * supply a tuple, it'd better have supplied a freefunc.
6220			 */
6221			if (HeapTupleIsValid(vardata->statsTuple) &&
6222				!vardata->freefunc)
6223				elog(ERROR, "no function provided to release variable stats with");
6224		}
6225		else
6226		{
6227			vardata->statsTuple = SearchSysCache3(STATRELATTINH,
6228												  ObjectIdGetDatum(relid),
6229												  Int16GetDatum(colnum),
6230												  BoolGetDatum(false));
6231			vardata->freefunc = ReleaseSysCache;
6232		}
6233	}
6234}
6235
6236/*
6237 * Check whether it is permitted to call func_oid passing some of the
6238 * pg_statistic data in vardata.  We allow this if either of the following
6239 * conditions is met: (1) the user has SELECT privileges on the table or
6240 * column underlying the pg_statistic data and there are no securityQuals from
6241 * security barrier views or RLS policies, or (2) the function is marked
6242 * leakproof.
6243 */
6244bool
6245statistic_proc_security_check(VariableStatData *vardata, Oid func_oid)
6246{
6247	if (vardata->acl_ok)
6248		return true;			/* have SELECT privs and no securityQuals */
6249
6250	if (!OidIsValid(func_oid))
6251		return false;
6252
6253	if (get_func_leakproof(func_oid))
6254		return true;
6255
6256	ereport(DEBUG2,
6257			(errmsg_internal("not using statistics because function \"%s\" is not leakproof",
6258							 get_func_name(func_oid))));
6259	return false;
6260}
6261
6262/*
6263 * get_variable_numdistinct
6264 *	  Estimate the number of distinct values of a variable.
6265 *
6266 * vardata: results of examine_variable
6267 * *isdefault: set to true if the result is a default rather than based on
6268 * anything meaningful.
6269 *
6270 * NB: be careful to produce a positive integral result, since callers may
6271 * compare the result to exact integer counts, or might divide by it.
6272 */
6273double
6274get_variable_numdistinct(VariableStatData *vardata, bool *isdefault)
6275{
6276	double		stadistinct;
6277	double		stanullfrac = 0.0;
6278	double		ntuples;
6279
6280	*isdefault = false;
6281
6282	/*
6283	 * Determine the stadistinct value to use.  There are cases where we can
6284	 * get an estimate even without a pg_statistic entry, or can get a better
6285	 * value than is in pg_statistic.  Grab stanullfrac too if we can find it
6286	 * (otherwise, assume no nulls, for lack of any better idea).
6287	 */
6288	if (HeapTupleIsValid(vardata->statsTuple))
6289	{
6290		/* Use the pg_statistic entry */
6291		Form_pg_statistic stats;
6292
6293		stats = (Form_pg_statistic) GETSTRUCT(vardata->statsTuple);
6294		stadistinct = stats->stadistinct;
6295		stanullfrac = stats->stanullfrac;
6296	}
6297	else if (vardata->vartype == BOOLOID)
6298	{
6299		/*
6300		 * Special-case boolean columns: presumably, two distinct values.
6301		 *
6302		 * Are there any other datatypes we should wire in special estimates
6303		 * for?
6304		 */
6305		stadistinct = 2.0;
6306	}
6307	else if (vardata->rel && vardata->rel->rtekind == RTE_VALUES)
6308	{
6309		/*
6310		 * If the Var represents a column of a VALUES RTE, assume it's unique.
6311		 * This could of course be very wrong, but it should tend to be true
6312		 * in well-written queries.  We could consider examining the VALUES'
6313		 * contents to get some real statistics; but that only works if the
6314		 * entries are all constants, and it would be pretty expensive anyway.
6315		 */
6316		stadistinct = -1.0;		/* unique (and all non null) */
6317	}
6318	else
6319	{
6320		/*
6321		 * We don't keep statistics for system columns, but in some cases we
6322		 * can infer distinctness anyway.
6323		 */
6324		if (vardata->var && IsA(vardata->var, Var))
6325		{
6326			switch (((Var *) vardata->var)->varattno)
6327			{
6328				case SelfItemPointerAttributeNumber:
6329					stadistinct = -1.0; /* unique (and all non null) */
6330					break;
6331				case TableOidAttributeNumber:
6332					stadistinct = 1.0;	/* only 1 value */
6333					break;
6334				default:
6335					stadistinct = 0.0;	/* means "unknown" */
6336					break;
6337			}
6338		}
6339		else
6340			stadistinct = 0.0;	/* means "unknown" */
6341
6342		/*
6343		 * XXX consider using estimate_num_groups on expressions?
6344		 */
6345	}
6346
6347	/*
6348	 * If there is a unique index, DISTINCT or GROUP-BY clause for the
6349	 * variable, assume it is unique no matter what pg_statistic says; the
6350	 * statistics could be out of date, or we might have found a partial
6351	 * unique index that proves the var is unique for this query.  However,
6352	 * we'd better still believe the null-fraction statistic.
6353	 */
6354	if (vardata->isunique)
6355		stadistinct = -1.0 * (1.0 - stanullfrac);
6356
6357	/*
6358	 * If we had an absolute estimate, use that.
6359	 */
6360	if (stadistinct > 0.0)
6361		return clamp_row_est(stadistinct);
6362
6363	/*
6364	 * Otherwise we need to get the relation size; punt if not available.
6365	 */
6366	if (vardata->rel == NULL)
6367	{
6368		*isdefault = true;
6369		return DEFAULT_NUM_DISTINCT;
6370	}
6371	ntuples = vardata->rel->tuples;
6372	if (ntuples <= 0.0)
6373	{
6374		*isdefault = true;
6375		return DEFAULT_NUM_DISTINCT;
6376	}
6377
6378	/*
6379	 * If we had a relative estimate, use that.
6380	 */
6381	if (stadistinct < 0.0)
6382		return clamp_row_est(-stadistinct * ntuples);
6383
6384	/*
6385	 * With no data, estimate ndistinct = ntuples if the table is small, else
6386	 * use default.  We use DEFAULT_NUM_DISTINCT as the cutoff for "small" so
6387	 * that the behavior isn't discontinuous.
6388	 */
6389	if (ntuples < DEFAULT_NUM_DISTINCT)
6390		return clamp_row_est(ntuples);
6391
6392	*isdefault = true;
6393	return DEFAULT_NUM_DISTINCT;
6394}
6395
6396/*
6397 * get_variable_range
6398 *		Estimate the minimum and maximum value of the specified variable.
6399 *		If successful, store values in *min and *max, and return true.
6400 *		If no data available, return false.
6401 *
6402 * sortop is the "<" comparison operator to use.  This should generally
6403 * be "<" not ">", as only the former is likely to be found in pg_statistic.
6404 * The collation must be specified too.
6405 */
6406static bool
6407get_variable_range(PlannerInfo *root, VariableStatData *vardata,
6408				   Oid sortop, Oid collation,
6409				   Datum *min, Datum *max)
6410{
6411	Datum		tmin = 0;
6412	Datum		tmax = 0;
6413	bool		have_data = false;
6414	int16		typLen;
6415	bool		typByVal;
6416	Oid			opfuncoid;
6417	FmgrInfo	opproc;
6418	AttStatsSlot sslot;
6419
6420	/*
6421	 * XXX It's very tempting to try to use the actual column min and max, if
6422	 * we can get them relatively-cheaply with an index probe.  However, since
6423	 * this function is called many times during join planning, that could
6424	 * have unpleasant effects on planning speed.  Need more investigation
6425	 * before enabling this.
6426	 */
6427#ifdef NOT_USED
6428	if (get_actual_variable_range(root, vardata, sortop, collation, min, max))
6429		return true;
6430#endif
6431
6432	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(vardata->statsTuple))
6433	{
6434		/* no stats available, so default result */
6435		return false;
6436	}
6437
6438	/*
6439	 * If we can't apply the sortop to the stats data, just fail.  In
6440	 * principle, if there's a histogram and no MCVs, we could return the
6441	 * histogram endpoints without ever applying the sortop ... but it's
6442	 * probably not worth trying, because whatever the caller wants to do with
6443	 * the endpoints would likely fail the security check too.
6444	 */
6445	if (!statistic_proc_security_check(vardata,
6446									   (opfuncoid = get_opcode(sortop))))
6447		return false;
6448
6449	opproc.fn_oid = InvalidOid; /* mark this as not looked up yet */
6450
6451	get_typlenbyval(vardata->atttype, &typLen, &typByVal);
6452
6453	/*
6454	 * If there is a histogram with the ordering we want, grab the first and
6455	 * last values.
6456	 */
6457	if (get_attstatsslot(&sslot, vardata->statsTuple,
6458						 STATISTIC_KIND_HISTOGRAM, sortop,
6459						 ATTSTATSSLOT_VALUES))
6460	{
6461		if (sslot.stacoll == collation && sslot.nvalues > 0)
6462		{
6463			tmin = datumCopy(sslot.values[0], typByVal, typLen);
6464			tmax = datumCopy(sslot.values[sslot.nvalues - 1], typByVal, typLen);
6465			have_data = true;
6466		}
6467		free_attstatsslot(&sslot);
6468	}
6469
6470	/*
6471	 * Otherwise, if there is a histogram with some other ordering, scan it
6472	 * and get the min and max values according to the ordering we want.  This
6473	 * of course may not find values that are really extremal according to our
6474	 * ordering, but it beats ignoring available data.
6475	 */
6476	if (!have_data &&
6477		get_attstatsslot(&sslot, vardata->statsTuple,
6478						 STATISTIC_KIND_HISTOGRAM, InvalidOid,
6479						 ATTSTATSSLOT_VALUES))
6480	{
6481		get_stats_slot_range(&sslot, opfuncoid, &opproc,
6482							 collation, typLen, typByVal,
6483							 &tmin, &tmax, &have_data);
6484		free_attstatsslot(&sslot);
6485	}
6486
6487	/*
6488	 * If we have most-common-values info, look for extreme MCVs.  This is
6489	 * needed even if we also have a histogram, since the histogram excludes
6490	 * the MCVs.  However, if we *only* have MCVs and no histogram, we should
6491	 * be pretty wary of deciding that that is a full representation of the
6492	 * data.  Proceed only if the MCVs represent the whole table (to within
6493	 * roundoff error).
6494	 */
6495	if (get_attstatsslot(&sslot, vardata->statsTuple,
6496						 STATISTIC_KIND_MCV, InvalidOid,
6497						 have_data ? ATTSTATSSLOT_VALUES :
6498						 (ATTSTATSSLOT_VALUES | ATTSTATSSLOT_NUMBERS)))
6499	{
6500		bool		use_mcvs = have_data;
6501
6502		if (!have_data)
6503		{
6504			double		sumcommon = 0.0;
6505			double		nullfrac;
6506			int			i;
6507
6508			for (i = 0; i < sslot.nnumbers; i++)
6509				sumcommon += sslot.numbers[i];
6510			nullfrac = ((Form_pg_statistic) GETSTRUCT(vardata->statsTuple))->stanullfrac;
6511			if (sumcommon + nullfrac > 0.99999)
6512				use_mcvs = true;
6513		}
6514
6515		if (use_mcvs)
6516			get_stats_slot_range(&sslot, opfuncoid, &opproc,
6517								 collation, typLen, typByVal,
6518								 &tmin, &tmax, &have_data);
6519		free_attstatsslot(&sslot);
6520	}
6521
6522	*min = tmin;
6523	*max = tmax;
6524	return have_data;
6525}
6526
6527/*
6528 * get_stats_slot_range: scan sslot for min/max values
6529 *
6530 * Subroutine for get_variable_range: update min/max/have_data according
6531 * to what we find in the statistics array.
6532 */
6533static void
6534get_stats_slot_range(AttStatsSlot *sslot, Oid opfuncoid, FmgrInfo *opproc,
6535					 Oid collation, int16 typLen, bool typByVal,
6536					 Datum *min, Datum *max, bool *p_have_data)
6537{
6538	Datum		tmin = *min;
6539	Datum		tmax = *max;
6540	bool		have_data = *p_have_data;
6541	bool		found_tmin = false;
6542	bool		found_tmax = false;
6543
6544	/* Look up the comparison function, if we didn't already do so */
6545	if (opproc->fn_oid != opfuncoid)
6546		fmgr_info(opfuncoid, opproc);
6547
6548	/* Scan all the slot's values */
6549	for (int i = 0; i < sslot->nvalues; i++)
6550	{
6551		if (!have_data)
6552		{
6553			tmin = tmax = sslot->values[i];
6554			found_tmin = found_tmax = true;
6555			*p_have_data = have_data = true;
6556			continue;
6557		}
6558		if (DatumGetBool(FunctionCall2Coll(opproc,
6559										   collation,
6560										   sslot->values[i], tmin)))
6561		{
6562			tmin = sslot->values[i];
6563			found_tmin = true;
6564		}
6565		if (DatumGetBool(FunctionCall2Coll(opproc,
6566										   collation,
6567										   tmax, sslot->values[i])))
6568		{
6569			tmax = sslot->values[i];
6570			found_tmax = true;
6571		}
6572	}
6573
6574	/*
6575	 * Copy the slot's values, if we found new extreme values.
6576	 */
6577	if (found_tmin)
6578		*min = datumCopy(tmin, typByVal, typLen);
6579	if (found_tmax)
6580		*max = datumCopy(tmax, typByVal, typLen);
6581}
6582
6583
6584/*
6585 * get_actual_variable_range
6586 *		Attempt to identify the current *actual* minimum and/or maximum
6587 *		of the specified variable, by looking for a suitable btree index
6588 *		and fetching its low and/or high values.
6589 *		If successful, store values in *min and *max, and return true.
6590 *		(Either pointer can be NULL if that endpoint isn't needed.)
6591 *		If unsuccessful, return false.
6592 *
6593 * sortop is the "<" comparison operator to use.
6594 * collation is the required collation.
6595 */
6596static bool
6597get_actual_variable_range(PlannerInfo *root, VariableStatData *vardata,
6598						  Oid sortop, Oid collation,
6599						  Datum *min, Datum *max)
6600{
6601	bool		have_data = false;
6602	RelOptInfo *rel = vardata->rel;
6603	RangeTblEntry *rte;
6604	ListCell   *lc;
6605
6606	/* No hope if no relation or it doesn't have indexes */
6607	if (rel == NULL || rel->indexlist == NIL)
6608		return false;
6609	/* If it has indexes it must be a plain relation */
6610	rte = root->simple_rte_array[rel->relid];
6611	Assert(rte->rtekind == RTE_RELATION);
6612
6613	/* ignore partitioned tables.  Any indexes here are not real indexes */
6614	if (rte->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE)
6615		return false;
6616
6617	/* Search through the indexes to see if any match our problem */
6618	foreach(lc, rel->indexlist)
6619	{
6620		IndexOptInfo *index = (IndexOptInfo *) lfirst(lc);
6621		ScanDirection indexscandir;
6622		StrategyNumber strategy;
6623
6624		/* Ignore non-ordering indexes */
6625		if (index->sortopfamily == NULL)
6626			continue;
6627
6628		/*
6629		 * Ignore partial indexes --- we only want stats that cover the entire
6630		 * relation.
6631		 */
6632		if (index->indpred != NIL)
6633			continue;
6634
6635		/*
6636		 * The index list might include hypothetical indexes inserted by a
6637		 * get_relation_info hook --- don't try to access them.
6638		 */
6639		if (index->hypothetical)
6640			continue;
6641
6642		/*
6643		 * get_actual_variable_endpoint uses the index-only-scan machinery, so
6644		 * ignore indexes that can't use it on their first column.
6645		 */
6646		if (!index->canreturn[0])
6647			continue;
6648
6649		/*
6650		 * The first index column must match the desired variable, sortop, and
6651		 * collation --- but we can use a descending-order index.
6652		 */
6653		if (collation != index->indexcollations[0])
6654			continue;			/* test first 'cause it's cheapest */
6655		if (!match_index_to_operand(vardata->var, 0, index))
6656			continue;
6657		strategy = get_op_opfamily_strategy(sortop, index->sortopfamily[0]);
6658		switch (IndexAmTranslateStrategy(strategy, index->relam, index->sortopfamily[0], true))
6659		{
6660			case COMPARE_LT:
6661				if (index->reverse_sort[0])
6662					indexscandir = BackwardScanDirection;
6663				else
6664					indexscandir = ForwardScanDirection;
6665				break;
6666			case COMPARE_GT:
6667				if (index->reverse_sort[0])
6668					indexscandir = ForwardScanDirection;
6669				else
6670					indexscandir = BackwardScanDirection;
6671				break;
6672			default:
6673				/* index doesn't match the sortop */
6674				continue;
6675		}
6676
6677		/*
6678		 * Found a suitable index to extract data from.  Set up some data that
6679		 * can be used by both invocations of get_actual_variable_endpoint.
6680		 */
6681		{
6682			MemoryContext tmpcontext;
6683			MemoryContext oldcontext;
6684			Relation	heapRel;
6685			Relation	indexRel;
6686			TupleTableSlot *slot;
6687			int16		typLen;
6688			bool		typByVal;
6689			ScanKeyData scankeys[1];
6690
6691			/* Make sure any cruft gets recycled when we're done */
6692			tmpcontext = AllocSetContextCreate(CurrentMemoryContext,
6693											   "get_actual_variable_range workspace",
6694											   ALLOCSET_DEFAULT_SIZES);
6695			oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(tmpcontext);
6696
6697			/*
6698			 * Open the table and index so we can read from them.  We should
6699			 * already have some type of lock on each.
6700			 */
6701			heapRel = table_open(rte->relid, NoLock);
6702			indexRel = index_open(index->indexoid, NoLock);
6703
6704			/* build some stuff needed for indexscan execution */
6705			slot = table_slot_create(heapRel, NULL);
6706			get_typlenbyval(vardata->atttype, &typLen, &typByVal);
6707
6708			/* set up an IS NOT NULL scan key so that we ignore nulls */
6709			ScanKeyEntryInitialize(&scankeys[0],
6710								   SK_ISNULL | SK_SEARCHNOTNULL,
6711								   1,	/* index col to scan */
6712								   InvalidStrategy, /* no strategy */
6713								   InvalidOid,	/* no strategy subtype */
6714								   InvalidOid,	/* no collation */
6715								   InvalidOid,	/* no reg proc for this */
6716								   (Datum) 0);	/* constant */
6717
6718			/* If min is requested ... */
6719			if (min)
6720			{
6721				have_data = get_actual_variable_endpoint(heapRel,
6722														 indexRel,
6723														 indexscandir,
6724														 scankeys,
6725														 typLen,
6726														 typByVal,
6727														 slot,
6728														 oldcontext,
6729														 min);
6730			}
6731			else
6732			{
6733				/* If min not requested, still want to fetch max */
6734				have_data = true;
6735			}
6736
6737			/* If max is requested, and we didn't already fail ... */
6738			if (max && have_data)
6739			{
6740				/* scan in the opposite direction; all else is the same */
6741				have_data = get_actual_variable_endpoint(heapRel,
6742														 indexRel,
6743														 -indexscandir,
6744														 scankeys,
6745														 typLen,
6746														 typByVal,
6747														 slot,
6748														 oldcontext,
6749														 max);
6750			}
6751
6752			/* Clean everything up */
6753			ExecDropSingleTupleTableSlot(slot);
6754
6755			index_close(indexRel, NoLock);
6756			table_close(heapRel, NoLock);
6757
6758			MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
6759			MemoryContextDelete(tmpcontext);
6760
6761			/* And we're done */
6762			break;
6763		}
6764	}
6765
6766	return have_data;
6767}
6768
6769/*
6770 * Get one endpoint datum (min or max depending on indexscandir) from the
6771 * specified index.  Return true if successful, false if not.
6772 * On success, endpoint value is stored to *endpointDatum (and copied into
6773 * outercontext).
6774 *
6775 * scankeys is a 1-element scankey array set up to reject nulls.
6776 * typLen/typByVal describe the datatype of the index's first column.
6777 * tableslot is a slot suitable to hold table tuples, in case we need
6778 * to probe the heap.
6779 * (We could compute these values locally, but that would mean computing them
6780 * twice when get_actual_variable_range needs both the min and the max.)
6781 *
6782 * Failure occurs either when the index is empty, or we decide that it's
6783 * taking too long to find a suitable tuple.
6784 */
6785static bool
6786get_actual_variable_endpoint(Relation heapRel,
6787							 Relation indexRel,
6788							 ScanDirection indexscandir,
6789							 ScanKey scankeys,
6790							 int16 typLen,
6791							 bool typByVal,
6792							 TupleTableSlot *tableslot,
6793							 MemoryContext outercontext,
6794							 Datum *endpointDatum)
6795{
6796	bool		have_data = false;
6797	SnapshotData SnapshotNonVacuumable;
6798	IndexScanDesc index_scan;
6799	Buffer		vmbuffer = InvalidBuffer;
6800	BlockNumber last_heap_block = InvalidBlockNumber;
6801	int			n_visited_heap_pages = 0;
6802	ItemPointer tid;
6803	Datum		values[INDEX_MAX_KEYS];
6804	bool		isnull[INDEX_MAX_KEYS];
6805	MemoryContext oldcontext;
6806
6807	/*
6808	 * We use the index-only-scan machinery for this.  With mostly-static
6809	 * tables that's a win because it avoids a heap visit.  It's also a win
6810	 * for dynamic data, but the reason is less obvious; read on for details.
6811	 *
6812	 * In principle, we should scan the index with our current active
6813	 * snapshot, which is the best approximation we've got to what the query
6814	 * will see when executed.  But that won't be exact if a new snap is taken
6815	 * before running the query, and it can be very expensive if a lot of
6816	 * recently-dead or uncommitted rows exist at the beginning or end of the
6817	 * index (because we'll laboriously fetch each one and reject it).
6818	 * Instead, we use SnapshotNonVacuumable.  That will accept recently-dead
6819	 * and uncommitted rows as well as normal visible rows.  On the other
6820	 * hand, it will reject known-dead rows, and thus not give a bogus answer
6821	 * when the extreme value has been deleted (unless the deletion was quite
6822	 * recent); that case motivates not using SnapshotAny here.
6823	 *
6824	 * A crucial point here is that SnapshotNonVacuumable, with
6825	 * GlobalVisTestFor(heapRel) as horizon, yields the inverse of the
6826	 * condition that the indexscan will use to decide that index entries are
6827	 * killable (see heap_hot_search_buffer()).  Therefore, if the snapshot
6828	 * rejects a tuple (or more precisely, all tuples of a HOT chain) and we
6829	 * have to continue scanning past it, we know that the indexscan will mark
6830	 * that index entry killed.  That means that the next
6831	 * get_actual_variable_endpoint() call will not have to re-consider that
6832	 * index entry.  In this way we avoid repetitive work when this function
6833	 * is used a lot during planning.
6834	 *
6835	 * But using SnapshotNonVacuumable creates a hazard of its own.  In a
6836	 * recently-created index, some index entries may point at "broken" HOT
6837	 * chains in which not all the tuple versions contain data matching the
6838	 * index entry.  The live tuple version(s) certainly do match the index,
6839	 * but SnapshotNonVacuumable can accept recently-dead tuple versions that
6840	 * don't match.  Hence, if we took data from the selected heap tuple, we
6841	 * might get a bogus answer that's not close to the index extremal value,
6842	 * or could even be NULL.  We avoid this hazard because we take the data
6843	 * from the index entry not the heap.
6844	 *
6845	 * Despite all this care, there are situations where we might find many
6846	 * non-visible tuples near the end of the index.  We don't want to expend
6847	 * a huge amount of time here, so we give up once we've read too many heap
6848	 * pages.  When we fail for that reason, the caller will end up using
6849	 * whatever extremal value is recorded in pg_statistic.
6850	 */
6851	InitNonVacuumableSnapshot(SnapshotNonVacuumable,
6852							  GlobalVisTestFor(heapRel));
6853
6854	index_scan = index_beginscan(heapRel, indexRel,
6855								 &SnapshotNonVacuumable, NULL,
6856								 1, 0);
6857	/* Set it up for index-only scan */
6858	index_scan->xs_want_itup = true;
6859	index_rescan(index_scan, scankeys, 1, NULL, 0);
6860
6861	/* Fetch first/next tuple in specified direction */
6862	while ((tid = index_getnext_tid(index_scan, indexscandir)) != NULL)
6863	{
6864		BlockNumber block = ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(tid);
6865
6866		if (!VM_ALL_VISIBLE(heapRel,
6867							block,
6868							&vmbuffer))
6869		{
6870			/* Rats, we have to visit the heap to check visibility */
6871			if (!index_fetch_heap(index_scan, tableslot))
6872			{
6873				/*
6874				 * No visible tuple for this index entry, so we need to
6875				 * advance to the next entry.  Before doing so, count heap
6876				 * page fetches and give up if we've done too many.
6877				 *
6878				 * We don't charge a page fetch if this is the same heap page
6879				 * as the previous tuple.  This is on the conservative side,
6880				 * since other recently-accessed pages are probably still in
6881				 * buffers too; but it's good enough for this heuristic.
6882				 */
6883#define VISITED_PAGES_LIMIT 100
6884
6885				if (block != last_heap_block)
6886				{
6887					last_heap_block = block;
6888					n_visited_heap_pages++;
6889					if (n_visited_heap_pages > VISITED_PAGES_LIMIT)
6890						break;
6891				}
6892
6893				continue;		/* no visible tuple, try next index entry */
6894			}
6895
6896			/* We don't actually need the heap tuple for anything */
6897			ExecClearTuple(tableslot);
6898
6899			/*
6900			 * We don't care whether there's more than one visible tuple in
6901			 * the HOT chain; if any are visible, that's good enough.
6902			 */
6903		}
6904
6905		/*
6906		 * We expect that the index will return data in IndexTuple not
6907		 * HeapTuple format.
6908		 */
6909		if (!index_scan->xs_itup)
6910			elog(ERROR, "no data returned for index-only scan");
6911
6912		/*
6913		 * We do not yet support recheck here.
6914		 */
6915		if (index_scan->xs_recheck)
6916			break;
6917
6918		/* OK to deconstruct the index tuple */
6919		index_deform_tuple(index_scan->xs_itup,
6920						   index_scan->xs_itupdesc,
6921						   values, isnull);
6922
6923		/* Shouldn't have got a null, but be careful */
6924		if (isnull[0])
6925			elog(ERROR, "found unexpected null value in index \"%s\"",
6926				 RelationGetRelationName(indexRel));
6927
6928		/* Copy the index column value out to caller's context */
6929		oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(outercontext);
6930		*endpointDatum = datumCopy(values[0], typByVal, typLen);
6931		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
6932		have_data = true;
6933		break;
6934	}
6935
6936	if (vmbuffer != InvalidBuffer)
6937		ReleaseBuffer(vmbuffer);
6938	index_endscan(index_scan);
6939
6940	return have_data;
6941}
6942
6943/*
6944 * find_join_input_rel
6945 *		Look up the input relation for a join.
6946 *
6947 * We assume that the input relation's RelOptInfo must have been constructed
6948 * already.
6949 */
6950static RelOptInfo *
6951find_join_input_rel(PlannerInfo *root, Relids relids)
6952{
6953	RelOptInfo *rel = NULL;
6954
6955	if (!bms_is_empty(relids))
6956	{
6957		int			relid;
6958
6959		if (bms_get_singleton_member(relids, &relid))
6960			rel = find_base_rel(root, relid);
6961		else
6962			rel = find_join_rel(root, relids);
6963	}
6964
6965	if (rel == NULL)
6966		elog(ERROR, "could not find RelOptInfo for given relids");
6967
6968	return rel;
6969}
6970
6971
6972/*-------------------------------------------------------------------------
6973 *
6974 * Index cost estimation functions
6975 *
6976 *-------------------------------------------------------------------------
6977 */
6978
6979/*
6980 * Extract the actual indexquals (as RestrictInfos) from an IndexClause list
6981 */
6982List *
6983get_quals_from_indexclauses(List *indexclauses)
6984{
6985	List	   *result = NIL;
6986	ListCell   *lc;
6987
6988	foreach(lc, indexclauses)
6989	{
6990		IndexClause *iclause = lfirst_node(IndexClause, lc);
6991		ListCell   *lc2;
6992
6993		foreach(lc2, iclause->indexquals)
6994		{
6995			RestrictInfo *rinfo = lfirst_node(RestrictInfo, lc2);
6996
6997			result = lappend(result, rinfo);
6998		}
6999	}
7000	return result;
7001}
7002
7003/*
7004 * Compute the total evaluation cost of the comparison operands in a list
7005 * of index qual expressions.  Since we know these will be evaluated just
7006 * once per scan, there's no need to distinguish startup from per-row cost.
7007 *
7008 * This can be used either on the result of get_quals_from_indexclauses(),
7009 * or directly on an indexorderbys list.  In both cases, we expect that the
7010 * index key expression is on the left side of binary clauses.
7011 */
7012Cost
7013index_other_operands_eval_cost(PlannerInfo *root, List *indexquals)
7014{
7015	Cost		qual_arg_cost = 0;
7016	ListCell   *lc;
7017
7018	foreach(lc, indexquals)
7019	{
7020		Expr	   *clause = (Expr *) lfirst(lc);
7021		Node	   *other_operand;
7022		QualCost	index_qual_cost;
7023
7024		/*
7025		 * Index quals will have RestrictInfos, indexorderbys won't.  Look
7026		 * through RestrictInfo if present.
7027		 */
7028		if (IsA(clause, RestrictInfo))
7029			clause = ((RestrictInfo *) clause)->clause;
7030
7031		if (IsA(clause, OpExpr))
7032		{
7033			OpExpr	   *op = (OpExpr *) clause;
7034
7035			other_operand = (Node *) lsecond(op->args);
7036		}
7037		else if (IsA(clause, RowCompareExpr))
7038		{
7039			RowCompareExpr *rc = (RowCompareExpr *) clause;
7040
7041			other_operand = (Node *) rc->rargs;
7042		}
7043		else if (IsA(clause, ScalarArrayOpExpr))
7044		{
7045			ScalarArrayOpExpr *saop = (ScalarArrayOpExpr *) clause;
7046
7047			other_operand = (Node *) lsecond(saop->args);
7048		}
7049		else if (IsA(clause, NullTest))
7050		{
7051			other_operand = NULL;
7052		}
7053		else
7054		{
7055			elog(ERROR, "unsupported indexqual type: %d",
7056				 (int) nodeTag(clause));
7057			other_operand = NULL;	/* keep compiler quiet */
7058		}
7059
7060		cost_qual_eval_node(&index_qual_cost, other_operand, root);
7061		qual_arg_cost += index_qual_cost.startup + index_qual_cost.per_tuple;
7062	}
7063	return qual_arg_cost;
7064}
7065
7066void
7067genericcostestimate(PlannerInfo *root,
7068					IndexPath *path,
7069					double loop_count,
7070					GenericCosts *costs)
7071{
7072	IndexOptInfo *index = path->indexinfo;
7073	List	   *indexQuals = get_quals_from_indexclauses(path->indexclauses);
7074	List	   *indexOrderBys = path->indexorderbys;
7075	Cost		indexStartupCost;
7076	Cost		indexTotalCost;
7077	Selectivity indexSelectivity;
7078	double		indexCorrelation;
7079	double		numIndexPages;
7080	double		numIndexTuples;
7081	double		spc_random_page_cost;
7082	double		num_sa_scans;
7083	double		num_outer_scans;
7084	double		num_scans;
7085	double		qual_op_cost;
7086	double		qual_arg_cost;
7087	List	   *selectivityQuals;
7088	ListCell   *l;
7089
7090	/*
7091	 * If the index is partial, AND the index predicate with the explicitly
7092	 * given indexquals to produce a more accurate idea of the index
7093	 * selectivity.
7094	 */
7095	selectivityQuals = add_predicate_to_index_quals(index, indexQuals);
7096
7097	/*
7098	 * If caller didn't give us an estimate for ScalarArrayOpExpr index scans,
7099	 * just assume that the number of index descents is the number of distinct
7100	 * combinations of array elements from all of the scan's SAOP clauses.
7101	 */
7102	num_sa_scans = costs->num_sa_scans;
7103	if (num_sa_scans < 1)
7104	{
7105		num_sa_scans = 1;
7106		foreach(l, indexQuals)
7107		{
7108			RestrictInfo *rinfo = (RestrictInfo *) lfirst(l);
7109
7110			if (IsA(rinfo->clause, ScalarArrayOpExpr))
7111			{
7112				ScalarArrayOpExpr *saop = (ScalarArrayOpExpr *) rinfo->clause;
7113				double		alength = estimate_array_length(root, lsecond(saop->args));
7114
7115				if (alength > 1)
7116					num_sa_scans *= alength;
7117			}
7118		}
7119	}
7120
7121	/* Estimate the fraction of main-table tuples that will be visited */
7122	indexSelectivity = clauselist_selectivity(root, selectivityQuals,
7123											  index->rel->relid,
7124											  JOIN_INNER,
